steps.title: Read the claim and identify what it requires
steps.content:
The underlined claim says male and female A. fasciatus share the same sponge. Supporting data must show both sexes present together in the same sponge — not just one sex, and not undetermined individuals.
  • Choice A — Each of the three sponges was inhabited by at least one male *A. fasciatus* and at least one female *A. fasciatus*.: directly shows both sexes in every sponge, proving shared microhabitat. ✓
  • Choice B — Sponge 1 was inhabited by 17 male *A. fasciatus*.: mentions only males in one sponge — no females shown, so shared habitat is not established. ✗
  • Choice C — Each of the three sponges was inhabited by at least one male *A. fasciatus* and at least one undetermined *A. fasciatus*.: the second group is undetermined sex, not confirmed female — does not prove male-female sharing. ✗
  • Choice D — Sponge 1 was inhabited by 13 female *A. fasciatus*.: mentions only females in one sponge — no males shown, so shared habitat is not established. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Only the matching choice provides data showing both a male and a female in each sponge, which directly supports the claim that the two sexes share the same microhabitat. A is correct.
